risk_title: Persistent High Political and Governance Risk
risk_summary: Political and governance risk remains rated as High across all assessment periods (approval, last approved rating, and current), indicating ongoing fragility in the operating environment that continues to affect project implementation in Ethiopia.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Political and Governance - Rating at Approval: High, Last Approved Rating: High, Proposed Rating: High

risk_summary: Macroeconomic risk has remained High throughout the project period, creating an unstable economic environment that affects project implementation and sustainability of livelihood interventions in the Horn of Africa region.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Macroeconomic - Rating at Approval: High, Last Approved Rating: High, Proposed Rating: High

risk_summary: Environment and Social risk has escalated from High at approval to High in current rating, with fiduciary risk also increasing from Substantial to High, indicating emerging or worsening social tensions and environmental pressures affecting implementation in displacement-affected areas.
evidence_quote: Environment and Social: Rating at Approval - High, Last Approved Rating - High, Proposed Rating - High; Fiduciary: Rating at Approval - Substantial, Last Approved Rating - High, Proposed Rating - High

risk_title: Refugee Population Pressure on Environmental Resources
risk_summary: The 1.1 million refugee population in Ethiopia is creating significant environmental degradation pressures that require intensive landscape management interventions, indicating displacement-driven environmental stress affecting project areas.
evidence_quote: to address land degradation and the environmental impact of the 1.1 million refugee population in Ethiopia, 21,800 hectares of land has been brought under sustainable landscape management practices
